# Quote templates

A quote template is a saved set of components you use together. When you start a new quote, pick the template and the components are already there. Then you measure.

Templates work with manual takeoff. They don't apply to digital takeoff because in digital takeoff you decide what to measure as you go.

## When to use them

- You quote the same kind of job over and over - standard residential re-roofs, sheds, lean-tos, commercial ridge replacements.
- You want a consistent set of components on every quote of that type.
- You want new staff to start with a known-good list.

## Steps to create one

1. Go to **Templates** in the workspace nav.
2. Click **New Quote Template**.
3. Name the template (e.g. "Standard residential re-roof - longrun").
4. Add the components you want included. Pick from your library.
5. Set defaults on each one - waste, pitch, rates - or leave them inheriting from the component.
6. Save.

## Steps to use one

1. Go to **Quotes > New Quote**.
2. Pick **Manual takeoff (roofing)**.
3. From the template dropdown, pick your template.
4. Continue. The components are pre-loaded.
5. Add your measurements per component as normal.

## What gets copied when you load a template

- Component list.
- Default waste, pitch, and rate settings (with any overrides you saved on the template).
- Notes on individual lines if you set any.

What doesn't get copied:

- Areas. You add those per quote.
- Measurements. You add those per quote.
- Customer info, job reference. Per quote.

## Tips

- Have one template per job type, not per customer. "Standard re-roof", "New build longrun", "Commercial ridge replacement" - not "John Smith" or "Bob's Bakery".
- Editing a template doesn't change quotes you've already built from it.
- Clone a template if you want a near-duplicate - faster than building from scratch.
